channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed

item.title: Big Walk was born during a COVID lockdown out of a distaste for birthday party Zoom calls
item.content:encoded:
"To have this little space where we could be together really meant a lot to us"

You know, I've been thinking about going for a nice big walk sometime soon. Well, not that soon, maybe sometime in the summer? Before I lock anything in, I'll just watch this new thing about House House's Big Walk and… oh, would you look at that! It's got a release date now, and for this summer too!

Summer being August 4th, a little under a couple of months away. A fresh look was offered at the multiplayer puzzle game and all the ways you can mess about and chat to your friends in appropriately goofy and delightful ways. I'm fond of how it's looking, but I've also been curious about where the idea came from in the first place, given House House's previous game Untitled Goose Game. Luckily, GamesIndustry.biz has a nifty little interview with the devs going into just that.

According to Stuart Gillespie-Cook of House House, a big driving force for the game was thanks to COVID lockdowns, and the ways we all had to learn to socialise with one another, including for birthdays. "I remember thinking about how I'd been to a bunch of Zoom birthday parties, which were horrible: just like 40 people in one Zoom call and only one person could talk at the time," Gillespie-Cook explains . "So very early on, one of the very earliest pictures was like, 'We'll just put [in] proximity voice chat, and it'll just be a space where we can meet with our friends'."

The first prototype for Big Walk was put together in about a week, leaving the team feeling like they really had something, admitting to being stuck indoors thanks to lockdown and missing one another factoring into that. "To have this little space where we could be together really meant a lot to us," Gillespie-Cook continued. "But once we tapped into that feeling, we were like, 'We need this game to be about this feeling.' And we've been chasing that for six years."

It's well worth reading the full interview, as the devs also go into that trendy new thing called friendslop, and their initial uncertainty in making Big Walk when there wasn't really a weird (even a semi ironic one like friendslop) to describe what they were making. And you can also read about what Edwin felt while playing it a while back, which was a whole lot of awkwardness, but in sort of a good way, actually.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/big-walk-was-born-during-a-covid-lockdown-out-of-a-distaste-for-birthday-party-zoom-calls
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Fallout 76 Devs Have No Plans For More Out of Map Expeditions
item.content:encoded:
Over the last few years, Bethesda Game Studios has tried to find new ways to broaden the adventure in Fallout 76. In one major way, they accomplished that through Expeditions, a system that took players away from the base map of Appalachia and into the great beyond.

To date, two expeditions have been added, one taking players to The Pitt, and another to Atlantic City. However, talking to Bethesda’s Jon Rush and Bill LaCoste at Summer Game Fest, I learned that there are no plans to add more Expeditions to Fallout 76 going forward, but there are opportunities to expand the base map even further.

No More Expeditions for Fallout 76 Players

Atlantic City and The Pitt offered solid content drops, giving players a way to explore Fallout 76 beyond the base map. In The Pitt, which was released in September 2022, players ventured back to a location introduced in a legendary Fallout 3 expansion. It was well received and was followed up almost two years later with Atlantic City, offering another out-of-map opportunity that was delivered in two parts.

However, since March 2024, Bethesda has stood silent on the future of Expeditions.

At Summer Game Fest, I asked Bethesda directly what the future of Expeditions looked like, and Jon Rush gave me a straight answer when I questioned if there were more in the pipeline:

For an out-of-map thing, no, for an in-map expansion, yes.

The issue with Expeditions is it’s fun content, it’s solid content, but there are some foundational issues with it that make people not want to continue engaging, right?

The base Appalachia map, Rush explained, is a layered, emergent environment where players can better influence what happens, and it naturally has more persistence and way more activities, particularly for those engaging in the endgame loop.

Expeditions, on the other hand, don’t really serve that deeper purpose.

You go in, you do these set things, and you get this, you go in, do you these set things, and you get this… Repeat, repeat, repeat.

Will there be more work for Expeditions down the road? Yes. Initially, not in the sense of ‘here’s a new place’, it’s more to fix that underlying issue to get people to want to engage with that again.

It could look like, ‘Hey, next two week, Expeditions in Atlantic City are open for everybody to go’, and then close it down, right? But change how the rewards are vended.

It’s a good point, because in recent months, the only reason I’ve visited Atlantic City or The Pitt is to grind some outstanding achievements that you can only get in those areas. Aside from that, I’ve got almost no reason to go there.

Short-term, there will be no new Expeditions in Fallout 76, and potentially long-term, too. What there will be are changes to how Expeditions work now to perhaps encourage players to lean on what exists.

Do you think there should be more Expeditions in Fallout 76? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that someone has remastered Fallout 3 using Unreal Engine

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/fallout-76-devs-no-more-out-of-map-expeditions/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Atelier Yumia: The Alchemist of Memories & the Envisioned Land – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition now available
item.content:encoded:
• Switch 2

A $10 upgrade from the Switch version.

Publisher Koei Tecmo and developer Gust have released Atelier Yumia: The Alchemist of Memories & the Envisioned Land – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition via Nintendo eShop. It is available at a 43 percent-off launch discount price of $39.89 until June 22, after which the price will change to $69.99. Users who own the Switch version can purchase an “Upgrade Pack” for $5.70 during the promotion period, or for $10 after it ends.

Atelier Yumia: The Alchemist of Memories & the Envisioned Land first lanuched for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, PlayStation 4, Xbox One, Switch, and PC via Steam on March 21, 2025 worldwide.

The Nintendo Switch 2 Edition includes the following benefits over the Switch version:

  • Improved resolution and framerate
  • Shorter loading times
  • New Local GameShare Functionality – While playing with another player using GameShare, you will be able to switch between the roles of main player and support as you battle and explore.
  • Includes the Atelier Yumia: Backpack Set – An accessory set for changing the color of Yumia’s backpack and Flammi’s smoke.

Watch the announcement trailer below.

Switch 2 Edition Trailer

English

Japanese



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/atelier-yumia-the-alchemist-of-memories-the-envisioned-land-nintendo-switch-2-edition-now-available
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Atelier Karia: The Night Kingdom & the Guide of Memories announced for PS5, Xbox Series, Switch 2, and PC
item.content:encoded:
Publisher Koei Tecmo and developer Gust have announced Atelier Karia: The Night Kingdom & the Guide of Memories, the next mainline entry in the Atelier series, for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, Switch 2, and PC (Steam). It will launch in early 2027 worldwide.

The teaser text reads, “A world wrapped in mystery located deep below Aladiss. What discoveries and revelations await Karia there?”

A live stream featuring more information on the game is planned for June 12 at 4:00 a.m. PT / 7:00 a.m. ET / 20:00 JST. You will be able to watch it on YouTube.

Watch the announcement trailer below. View the first screenshots at the gallery. Visit the official website here: English / Japanese.

Announce Trailer

English

Japanese

Screenshots

  • ❮
  • ❯


https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/atelier-karia-the-night-kingdom-the-guide-of-memories-announced-for-ps5-xbox-series-switch-2-and-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Nintendo of Europe to pay 40m fine for Joy-Con drift issues
item.content:encoded:
Nintendo of Europe has agreed to pay a €35 million fine (around $40 million) after a French regulator claimed the company misled customers about Joy-Con drift defects on the original Nintendo Switch controllers.

The news was reported by Le Monde yesterday (thanks, GamesIndustry.Biz), sharing details about the culmination of an investigation by France's General Directorate for Competition, Consumer Affairs, and Fraud Control (DGCCRF).

The investigation found that Nintendo was aware of technical defects, which included responsiveness issues and drift, as early as 2018. Yet, the company did not inform consumers until 2020, two years later.

The DGCCRF said this led to consumers buying unnecessary replacement controllers. The National Investigation Service (SNE) of the DGCCRF determined that Nintendo of Europe misled consumers by failing to disclose Joy-Con controller defects transparently.

Nintendo of Europe said in a statement that it did not "intentionally mislead consumers." Moreover, the company stated that agreeing to the settlement "does not constitute an admission of guilt and reflects only the amicable resolution of legal proceedings."

Related:Xbox is rethinking strategy around Project Helix thanks to the memory crisis

The investigation originally began after a 2020 complaint from the French consumer association UFC-Que Choisir, the same group that has sued the likes of Ubisoft and Valve over the years.

When the complaints from European consumer right groups first started, the aim was to open a dialogue with Nintendo that would encourage the company to address the drift issue. They weren't the only ones. A mere year before, a class-action lawsuit in the United States collected multiple testimonies from Switch owners that had ran into trouble with their Joy-Cons, including one individual that said he encountered the issue with both new and freshly repaired controllers. That lawsuit was dismissed in 2024.

About the Author

Contributing Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Diego Nicolás Argüello is a freelance journalist and critic from Argentina. Video games helped him to learn English, so now he covers them for places like The New York Times, NPR, Rolling Stone, and more. He also runs Into the Spine, a site dedicated to fostering and supporting new writers, and co-hosted Turnabout Breakdown, a podcast about the Ace Attorney series. He’s most likely playing a rhythm game as you read this.



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/nintendo-of-europe-to-pay-40m-fine-for-joy-con-drift-issues
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Dragon's Dogma 2 Announces Dark Arisen Expansion, Adding New Areas, Dungeons, And Cosmetics This October
item.content:encoded:
Dragon's Dogma 2, a challenging, friction-filled fantasy adventure from Capcom, was one of my favorite games back in 2024. Today, during Nintendo's big summer Direct, we learned that the game is coming to Switch 2. What was less clear from the Direct's trailer, however, is that the Switch 2 version is actually Dragon's Dogma 2: Dark Arisen, an updated release of the game that will come bundled with the Dark Arisen expansion. That expansion is also coming as paid DLC to every other console the game is currently on, and you can see the trailer for it below:

Dark Arisen's main selling point are the frozen lands of Norgan, a new icy area to the far north that players can explore for powerful relics, which can be appraised to unlock new gear and skills. The expansion also adds 12 new dungeon challenges and new hair and tattoo options to customize players and their pawns.

There are also free updates on the way for the base game, and according to the new trailer's video description, the first one arrives today. "The first arrives June 9 with quality-of-life improvements, including the addition of an Eternal Ferrystone and more Portcrystals," it reads. "A second update in August brings framerate and performance improvements to all platforms, additional save slots, and many more adjustments and improvements." The Eternal Ferrystone sounds like it will remove some of the fast travel restrictions that put people off from the base game, even though I think friction makes the game better, I don't mind an update that might welcome new players.

Dragon's Dogma II: Dark Arisen is out four months from today, on October 9, for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X.S, PC, and Switch 2, and if you pre-order the game, you'll get access to the Northern Attire fashion set. For more Dragon's Dogma, check out our review of the base game.



https://gameinformer.com/2026/06/09/dragons-dogma-2-announces-dark-arisen-expansion-adding-new-areas-dungeons-and-cosmetics
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: AphroditE’s Stardom Reunion Is Gay As Hell
item.content:encoded:
Professional wrestling is a love story. Sometimes that love story is eternal, like the Golden Lovers, Rhiyo, and whatever Triple H and Shawn Michaels have going on, and other times it's toxic as hell. The current love story in the Japanese women’s wrestling organization World Wonder Ring Stardom is straight cinema of the toxic yuri variety, because it so effectively blurs the line between kayfabe and reality to enhance its doomed romance. But to get to why it's the most exciting thing in wrestling right now, allow me to turn back the clock and explain their lore.

You see, Saya Kamitami had a problem. Back in 2024, Utami Hayashita announced her departure from Stardom. While they were in Stardom together, Hayashita was the faction leader of Queen’s Quest, one of the leading and oldest surviving factions of Stardom before its wrestlers departed for AEW and WWE. Within Queen’s Quest, Hayashita and Kamitani formed their own duo tag team, AphroditE. Although Kamitani was more technically skilled than Hayashita, she never got as over as her fearless leader, what with how handsome she was, with her Tuxedo Mask-esque gimmick of gifting roses to women in the audience and wooing fellow wrestlers. 

Although it's not like the two never squared off against each other, there was always something unspoken about them. An "always the bridesmaid, never the bride" type of situation. 

Still, the two formed an unbreakable bond to the point where they even drunkenly kissed one night out together and thought nothing big of it as a testament to their closeness. Granted, ladies kissing each other in Stardom is hardly a rare phenomenon. It’s been used to send wrestlers off when they graduate from the company, just as it's been used as a kiss of death in the lead up to a big match—the latter of which had Kamitani in her feelings when Syuri, the Japanese women’s wrestling boogeyman, set her sights on Hayashita. AphroditE is basically the wrestling equivalent of T.A.T.U.

Against Kamitani’s protests, Hayashita left Stardom, leaving a void for Kamitani to fill, while Hayashita became the wrestler for former Stardom promoter Rossy Ogawa to build around his newly formed company, Dream Star Fighting Marigold. Outside of becoming Marigold’s big champion and putting on a clinic with former Queen’s Quest leader and WWE superstar Iyo Sky, Hayashita mostly faded into obscurity in Marigold. A phenomenon that, in my humble opinion, Rossy’s disgusting public fat-shaming of Hayashita bears significant blame for, but I digress. Fuck that guy, by the way. 

Meanwhile, Kamitani was struggling in Stardom. In a similar fashion to WWE pushing Roman Reigns by any means necessary and not getting over with the crowd, Kamitani also struggled to stand out from Hayashita’s shadow as the next big thing. It got so bad that she was harassed by fans at an autograph signing—leading her to swear off attending future fan meet-and-greets.

But from that experience—be it a work or otherwise—came an opportunity for Kamitani to rebuild herself as the Phenex Queen of Stardom. Gone was her golden-trimmed green-and-white gear, replaced by dark sequins and heavy dark eye makeup. She finally turned heel. 

What’s more, she started her own faction of wrestlers born from the flames of her own harassment from the fans that rejected her as Stardom’s next big thing: H.A.T.E. (Harassment, Abuse, Terrorize, Eradicate). Her heel persona, a send-up of heels of yesteryear like Dump Matsumoto, absolutely terrorized Stardom. With her new persona, Saya was able to get the edge on her opponents, not by changing her wheelhouse of high-flying moves as most wrestlers do, but by adding more animosity and spite into her moves. 

Her meteoric rise not only saw her retire Tam Nakano, one of, if not the last vestige of Stardom’s era when Hayashita was around, but it also saw her finally standing at the top of the promotion. She became a measuring stick rookies would try and fail to best, all the while, she’d giddily laugh at them from on high. Then, everything changed when Hayashita returned to Stardom. 

Their reunion saw Kamitani face a ghost of her past. One she’d eclipsed when Hayashita was at her peak, sure. But Hayashita also served as a grim reminder of the weak girl Kamitani was before. After all, the woman she’d cling to like a child and cried over her leaving made a surprise return. Not only that, Hayashita said she’d never kept her eyes off Kamitani, even when she was at another promotion. And that sting hurt all the more because Hayashita wasn’t even jockeying for a title shot against Kamitani. 

Despite being a legend in her own right, she wanted to start fresh by working her way up by wrestling the rookies. But to Kamitani that meant their reunion would be prolonged. Their fateful meeting depended on Hayashita’s success in a Stardom she’d leveled  Kamitani’s dominance backfired, turning into a new twisted version of her still being the bridesmaid to Hayashita’s career while she gave other wrestlers her attention in the ring. Girlie is suffering from her own success and she’s seething. 

What makes AphroditE’s reunion the best thing in wrestling right now is that it’s a testament to the medium’s uncanny ability to tap into soap opera-esque long term storytelling. It’s storytelling that has just as much to do with what’s seen on screen as it does to the real life circumstances, and both those elements dictate how events play out. These are all things that viewers are tapped into, and they elevate wrestling’s storytelling when it blurs the line between real life and fiction in a way only it can. 

It’s why a mid-card wrestler’s parody of a luchador gimmick can get him more over than Goku in Mexico. It’s why the debut of one of the biggest wrestlers from a rival company leads to a paradigm shift in wrestling. And it's why rivalries like Hayashita and Kamitani’s, built on years of yearning, bring new meaning to their eventual reunion, now colored by their history inside and outside of the ring. 

Recommended



https://aftermath.site/stardom-utami-hayashita-saya-kamitani-aphrodite-toxic-yuri-joshi-puroresu/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Fire Emblem: Fortune's Weave Price And Box Art Revealed
item.content:encoded:
Lookin' good

With Nintendo now confirming that Fire Emblem: Fortune's Weave will launch for the Switch 2 on 17th September 2026, it's also graced us with the official box art.

Let's start with the price. Fortune's Weave will cost $69.99 on the North American eShop and £58.99 in the UK. Physical is likely to be a bit more expensive for both regions, but we'll confirm this as soon as we can.

And then we have the box art.

We won't beat around the bush with this one; we know you all want to check out the goods, so here it is!

Looks nice, huh? Of course, a special edition will also be available if you want to go all-out, and we'll confirm the price for that one as soon as we can.

Pretty standard price for this one. The box art looks great though. Share your thoughts on both with a comment down below.

Nintendo Life’s resident horror fanatic, when he’s not knee-deep in Resident Evil and Silent Hill lore, Ollie likes to dive into a good horror book while nursing a lovely cup of tea. He also enjoys long walks and listens to everything from TOOL to Chuck Berry.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply


https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/06/fire-emblem-fortunes-weave-price-and-box-art-revealed
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Rhythm Heaven Groove's Multiplayer Modes Look Like A Game Night Essential
item.content:encoded:
Feel the beat

Keen to make sure no one missed any hot Rhythm Heaven Groove content, Nintendo plonked it right at the very start of today's Direct, and it continues to look wonderful.

Today's new overview trailer gave us a taste of some of the 80 new rhythm minigames available in Groove, but the thing that really caught our eye was the multiplayer modes, which will be slotting straight into our game night rotations.

In these modes, you can play with friends across "over 30 co-operative and competitive games", and they look like a right laugh. Seeing who can slice away arrows to the beat, grab a slice of cake, or survive an onslaught of giant rolling balls? Yes please.

There's also an ace-looking single-player mode, where you'll have to use the rhythm to cast spells and make your way through a series of dungeon-crawling battles. It vaguely reminded us of the good old StreetPass Quest days, and that's never a bad thing!

As a reminder, Rhythm Heaven Groove arrives on Switch on 2nd July.

What do you make of the latest Rhythm Heaven Groove trailer? Type to the beat in the comments and let us know.

Jim came to Nintendo Life in 2022 and, despite his insistence that The Minish Cap is the best Zelda game and his unwavering love for the Star Wars prequels (yes, really), he has continued to write news and features on the site ever since.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply


https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/06/rhythm-heaven-grooves-multiplayer-modes-look-like-a-game-night-essential
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The full Kingdom Hearts saga is coming to Switch 2, PS5 and Xbox Series X|S
item.content:encoded:
Square Enix has announced that the full Kingdom Hearts saga so far is being upgraded and ported to PS5, Xbox Series X|S and Nintendo Switch 2, launching on 8th October 2026.

As part of this, the KINGDOM HEARTS Collection [I~III] will be sold as an all-in-one bundle for people that want the whole lot,  but you can also buy each chapter separately. That’s KINGDOM HEARTS -HD 1.5+2.5 ReMIX-, KINGDOM HEARTS HD 2.8 Final Chapter Prologue and KINGDOM HEARTS III + Re Mind (DLC).

I know I’m not the first one to say this, but the way these remastered collections have been named over the years is beyond ridiculous.

The big news for Switch 2 buyers is that these will now be running natively on the console, where the Switch 1 releases were all ‘Cloud ver.’ and played remotely. That was Goofy at the time and it’s still pretty quackers, if you ask Donald me. A demo of Kingdom Hearts 3 is available today for Switch 2 featuring the start of the game through to Olympus (with save data that can carry across to the full game), and then jumping ahead to Toy Box World (where save data will not carry over)

Upgrade pricing is also available, with a 50% discount on digital purchases from last gen to Switch 2, PS5 and Xbox Series X|S. Switch 1 to Switch 2 will also be able to transfer save data, but this is a mysteriously absent feature on Xbox and PlayStation. I see no good reason why it shouldn’t be possible, given that there’s countless other games that have a save transfer between generations.

Square Enix also released a new teaser trailer for Kingdom Hearts IV, confirming that it will now also be coming to Switch 2.



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/09/the-full-kingdom-hearts-saga-is-coming-to-switch-2-ps5-and-xbox-series-xs/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Pikuniku 2 enters the third dimension in 2027
item.content:encoded:
The puzzle-exploration sequel Pikuniku 2 has been revealed, and this time its quirky adventure is in 3D.

Pikuniku 2 is coming to PC and Switch 2 in 2027

Pikuniku 2 starts with Piku and friends going off on a picnic, only for a sandwich to escape, a boat to get wrecked, and everyone to get split up across an archipelago. So it’s up to Piku to go and find everyone.

Along the way, Piku will meet a bunch of strange people, hear their stories – including stuff about giant fruit, evil robot frogs and an all-powerful potato. There’s also a new deep state conspiracy to unravel.

I thoroughly enjoyed the original Pikuniku, which launched some seven years ago. Miguel also loved it, and said in our Pikuniku review, “My favorite gaming experiences are the ones that elicit a pure and physical response from me. Whether it’s a horror game making me clench my cheeks or a story-driven game dropping my jaw and pulling a tear from my eye, those kinds of experiences are the ones that stick with me the most. With Pikuniku, I was smiling and laughing throughout the entire thing, and too few games get that kind of response out of me. Pikuniku is a treasure of a game, and I can’t wait to see more from this team.”



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/09/pikuniku-2-enters-the-third-dimension-in-2027/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Kingdom Hearts 4 Gameplay Emerges After Years of Silence, KH 1-3 Collection Coming to Nintendo Switch 2 in October 2026
item.content:encoded:
While today's Nintendo Direct was stuffed mostly with ports of games previously released elsewhere and a few new titles and DLC updates sprinkled in between, it capped off with a couple of major reveals. One was the expected reveal of The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time Remake, while the other was another look at Kingdom Hearts 4 after years of silence, alongside a reveal that the rest of the series will make its way to Nintendo Switch 2 in one jam-packed collection.

Kingdom Hearts 4 was first announced back in 2022, and since then we've barely seen any updates on the game since. A couple images of concept art and a comment from Square Enix that the game was "on schedule" in September 2025 is about all we've heard of the game in the last four years. Now, we actually have more than just a cinematic trailer to point to as proof that the game is being made and is on its way.

We didn't get any hint towards a release window, but we did get confirmation that it'll arrive on Nintendo Switch 2 at launch, with a press release afterwards adding that it'll also arrive on PC, PS5, and Xbox Series X/S.

What we did get a release date for however, is the big Kingdom Hearts collection that'll be coming to Nintendo Switch 2. A demo is available now for Switch 2 owners to check the collection out, which will show off a bit of Kingdom Hearts 3, starting you off at the beginning of the game in one phase of the demo and then skipping ahead to the middle of the game for the second phase of the demo.

As for what's included in the collection, Square Enix left no stone unturned as players will be able to dive into:

  • KINGDOM HEARTS -HD 1.5+2.5 ReMIX- (Digital only)
    • KINGDOM HEARTS FINAL MIX
    • KINGDOM HEARTS Re:Chain of Memories
    • KINGDOM HEARTS II FINAL MIX
    • KINGDOM HEARTS 358/2 Days (HD cutscene compilation)
    • KINGDOM HEARTS Birth by Sleep FINAL MIX
    • KINGDOM HEARTS Re:coded (HD cutscene compilation)
  • KINGDOM HEARTS HD 2.8 Final Chapter Prologue (Digital only)
    • KINGDOM HEARTS Dream Drop Distance HD
    • KINGDOM HEARTS χ Back Cover (movie)
    • KINGDOM HEARTS 0.2 Birth By Sleep
  • KINGDOM HEARTS III + Re Mind (DLC) (Digital only)
    • KINGDOM HEARTS III
    • KINGDOM HEARTS Re Mind (DLC)
  • KINGDOM HEARTS Collection [I~III] (Physical and Digital)
    • All games from the KINGDOM HEARTS -HD 1.5+2.5 ReMIX-, KINGDOM HEARTS HD 2.8 Final Chapter Prologue and KINGDOM HEARTS III + Re Mind (DLC) bundles

The Kingdom Hearts Collection will arrive on October 8, 2026, which will likely give you plenty of time to play through each game and its various additional bits ahead of Kingdom Hearts 4, since it seems like we'll still be waiting quite a while before it is ready to release.

About the author: David has been writing about videogames, technology, and culture since 2020, with a focus on reporting daily news across multiple publications, including GameDaily.Biz, GameSkinny, and PlayStation Universe before joining Wccftech in 2025. David started contributing as Canada/US reporter for Wccftech's gaming section in 2025. Besides being up-to-date on the industry's movements, he loves interviewing developers, reviewing games, and writing intricate essays about the symbolism and layered meanings to be found in rich narratives as he's done for publications like GamesIndustry.Biz, LostInCult, and others. Outside of games he loves movies, music, theatre, his hometown, and his family, though not necessarily in that order.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/kingdom-hearts-4-gameplay-kingdom-hearts-collection-release-date-nintendo-switch-2/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Reckless Ben Shares ‘Final Message’ Regarding Episode 3 Of Bricks & Minifigs Lego Drama: ‘I Can’t Post It, Or Else I’ll Go To Jail’
item.content:encoded:
Reckless Ben’s investigation into the Mansell family’s missing $200,000 Lego Star Wars collection has hit a seemingly immovable force, as he’s announced that he’s now no longer able to release the third and final part of his series regarding Lego resellers Bricks & Minifigs after being served a TRO (temporary restraining order) by the Utah Fourth Judicial District Court.

The last time we left Reckless Ben’s Lego investigation, things were seemingly looking up for both Ben and the Mansell family. The CEO of Patreon stepped in to personally ensure that Reckless Ben’s content would remain on the platform, unredacted bodycam footage of American Fork Police Department’s involvement had allegedly leaked online, and the GoFundMe set up to reimburse the Mansell family for their missing Lego Star Wars collection had reached over $450,000.

Given the way things seemed to be going, Ben’s fans are understandably not too happy about the latest development in his crusade against Bricks & Minifigs Franchising (BAMF). As Ben explained in the latest video on his channel, published earlier today on June 9, a judge has allowed BAMF to serve him with legal papers via his email address, which means he can no longer publish (or even talk about) the final part of his investigation.

As Ben explained in his latest video, releasing Part 3 of his investigation wouldn’t just get him in legal trouble, it would also result in the loss of the Mansell family’s GoFundMe: “Normally I would be like, ‘screw the big guy telling me what to do, I’m gonna do what’s morally right.’ But, in this situation, if I do that, then all my friends get screwed with this lawsuit, and we lose all this GoFundMe money we raised, like, immediately.”

He’s not bluffing either, as the TRO, dated May 28, 2026, specifically states that Ben can no longer release any videos relating to Bricks & Minifigs Franchising. In fact, it specifically states that even Ben’s previous videos may be removed as well, due to the fact that they contain information that relates “to the private legal dispute underlying this matter between Bryan [Mansell] and Chrystal [Law, one of the previous owners of the Bricks & Minifigs location in Salem, Oregon] and the assertions of wrongdoing as described in this Verified Complaint.”

Still, I have a feeling this isn’t the end for Reckless Ben’s investigation. After all, he doesn’t seem like the type to let something like a court-issued TRO stop him. Considering the CEO of Patreon was so keen to help last week, maybe he could post it on Ben’s behalf?



https://kotaku.com/reckless-ben-lego-star-wars-part-3-investigation-bricks-minifigs-2000704363
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The Duskbloods closed network test set for summer
item.content:encoded:
• Switch 2

FromSoftware's multiplayer action game still due out in 2026.

FromSoftware will host a closed network test for player-versus-player-versus-environment-based multiplayer action game The Duskbloods this summer, the developer announced.

Here are the latest details, via Nintendo:

In this new multiplayer action game, you’ll take on the role of the Bloodsworn and fight against—and alongside—up to seven other players with unique weapons and abilities. Transcend humanity with blood-based powers that transform your character and combat style as you battle to secure the coveted First Blood. The Duskbloods launches exclusively on Nintendo Switch 2 in 2026—and as revealed in the presentation, players can learn more about the closed network test for the game, coming in summer 2026!

The Duskbloods is due out for Switch 2 in 2026.

Watch a new trailer below.

Closed Network Test Trailer

English

Japanese



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/the-duskbloods-closed-network-test-set-for-summer
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: HD-2D RPG Final Fantasy Resonance announced for PS5, Xbox Series, Switch 2, Switch, and PC
item.content:encoded:
Publisher and developer Square Enix, and developer Lancarse have announced Final Fantasy Resonance, the first HD-2D RPG in the Final Fantasy series, for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, Switch 2, Switch, and PC (Steam, Microsoft Store). It will launch on October 22.

Pre-orders are available now in the following editions:

  • Standard Edition ($49.99 / £49.99 / €59.99 / ¥7,678)
    • A copy of the game
    • Pre-order bonus:
      • Magitek Airship & Squire Starter Pack – Contains an item that transforms your airship’s appearance into a magitek vessel, along with useful items to aid you at the start of your journey.
        • Magitek Airship Passkey (x1)
        • Knight’s Greatsword (x1)
        • Chestplate of Preparation (x1) (Increases EXP gained in battle by a certain amount)
        • Magicite Shard (x1)
        • Tent (x3)
        • Mist Potion (x3)
    • Early purchase bonus:
      • Squire Kickstart Pack – This set contains useful items to aid you at the start of your journey.
        • Blessed Cuirass (x1) (Increase gil obtained by a certain amount)
        • Mist Ether (x1)
  • Digital Deluxe Edition ($59.99 / £54.99 / €69.99 / ¥9,878)
    • A copy of the game
    • Magitek & Grimoire Deluxe Pack – Contains an item that transforms your chocobo’s appearance into magitek armor, an item exclusive to this pack that unlocks a certain character’s Fel Meteor Limit Burst, and useful items to aid you at the start of your journey.
      • Magitek Armor Key (x1)
      • Archwitch’s Grimoire (x1)
      • Mastery Ring (x1)
      • Tent (x5)
      • Mist Potion (x5)
      • Mist Ether (x5)
      • Phoenix Down (x5)
      • Remedy (x3)
    • Pre-order bonus:
      • Magitek Airship & Squire Starter Pack – Contains an item that transforms your airship’s appearance into a magitek vessel, along with useful items to aid you at the start of your journey.
        • Magitek Airship Passkey (x1)
        • Knight’s Greatsword (x1)
        • Chestplate of Preparation (x1) (Increases EXP gained in battle by a certain amount)
        • Magicite Shard (x1)
        • Tent (x3)
        • Mist Potion (x3)
    • Early purchase bonus:
      • Squire Kickstart Pack – This set contains useful items to aid you at the start of your journey.
        • Blessed Cuirass (x1) (Increase gil obtained by a certain amount)
        • Mist Ether (x1)

Here is an overview of the game, via Square Enix:

About

Return to the pixel‑art world of Final Fantasy, evolved from its classic roots. Travel with companions bound by fate and call on Visions to aid you in battle to uncover the truth behind a force that threatens to bring everything to ruin.

Key Features

  • Cinematic Pixel Art – What if Final Fantasy had continued to push the limits of pixel art? The first Final Fantasy to join the HD‑2D lineup, Final Fantasy Resonance delivers a visually striking experience that feels both nostalgic and new, enhanced by dynamic camera angles and pixelized 3D models that make every moment more dramatic and immersive.
  • A Return to the Familiar – A classic RPG adventure unfolds in a world filled with beloved FINAL FANTASY elements—airships, crystals, espers and moogles woven into the fabric of the story, and so much more.
  • Strategic Turn-Based Battles – Exploit enemy weaknesses to stagger them—breaking their defenses to seize extra turns and gain the upper hand with powerful Resonance attacks in cinematic battles shaped by smart choices.
  • Final Fantasy Legends – Bolster your team with Visions, echoes of beloved Final Fantasy characters, who fight alongside you with unique skills and abilities for deep party customization.
  • Hours of Side Content to Discover – The world is full of secrets, including the wandering swordmaster Gilgamesh, a Colosseum with deadly monsters and rewards to match, the Chamber of Arms with formidable foes sealed within legendary weapons, and a showdown with Ultima Weapon.

Watch the announcement trailer and a message from the producer below. Visit the official website here: English / Japanese.

Announce Trailer

English

Japanese

Producer Message

Japanese



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/hd-2d-rpg-final-fantasy-resonance-announced-for-ps5-xbox-series-switch-2-switch-and-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Devil May Cry 5 Devil Hunter Edition announced for Switch 2
item.content:encoded:
• Switch 2

Due out on June 23.

Capcom has announced Devil May Cry 5 Devil Hunter Edition for Switch 2. It will launch digitally on June 23 and physically on August 28.

Devil May Cry 5 first launched for PlayStation 4, Xbox One, and PC via Steam on March 8, 2019, followed by Devil May Cry 5 Special Edition for Xbox Series on November 10, 2020, and PlayStation 5 on November 12, 2020.

Here is an overview of the game, via Capcom:

The award-winning Devil May Cry 5 is coming to Nintendo Switch 2 in the feature-packed Devil May Cry 5 Devil Hunter Edition with pre-orders available now! Play as Nero, Dante, V, or Vergil in high-octane battles as they carve a path through hellish opponents using unique fighting styles, weapons, and stylish moves. Devil May Cry 5 Devil Hunter Edition comes packed with the Vergil playable character downloadable content in addition to robust bonus content including costume colours, taunts, and battle tracks that you can enjoy anytime, anywhere in a smooth 60 frames-per-second across both TV and handheld modes.

The digital version of Devil May Cry 5 Devil Hunter Edition will be available on June 23, 2026. Players who are motivated to pre-order or acquire the digital version of Devil May Cry 5 Devil Hunter Edition by July 7, 2026 will be eligible for a discount. The physical version will be available for pre-order and / or purchase at select retailers and will be available on August 28, 2026.

Watch a new trailer below. View a set of screenshots at the gallery.

Switch 2 Announce Trailer

English

Screenshots

  • ❮
  • ❯


https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/devil-may-cry-5-devil-hunter-edition-announced-for-switch-2
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Xbox is rethinking strategy around Project Helix thanks to the memory crisis
item.content:encoded:
Matthew Ball, Xbox's chief strategy officer, said the ongoing memory crisis is not getting any better, in the light of Project Helix, the company's upcoming console.

In an interview at The Game Business Live this past weekend, via GameSpot, Ball spoke about the plans for the console amidst the memory chip supply crisis—driven by rampant investment in artificial intelligence data centers (thanks, Reuters).

"The window in which we and others are gonna have to work through is getting longer, and that is going to constrain the category," he said.

Ball said that he previously underestimated how bad things might get. In late April, the newly-appointed Xbox CEO Asha Sharma said key component shortages could impact the price and availability of next-generation hardware.

At the time, Sharma said the company would be taking the potential impact into consideration, and that the company was not ready to share a launch timeline for the moment, mentioning that "the world's pretty dynamic."

Related:007: First Light sales hit 3M in less than two weeks

Now, based on the conversation with Ball, demand for Xbox consoles is outstripping supply. The chief strategy officer said Xbox is producing them "as quickly as possible," while adding that "there is a severe limitation to how quickly we can do that."

Speaking about Helix specifically, Ball said, "We are working very hard to rethink everything that we can about Helix, which is a console we are committed to shipping."

For the time being, Xbox is rethinking what the console model can look like, and trying to remain flexible. Ball also mentioned the crisis may have acute effects for two or two and a half years.

"We are working very hard to figure out the best way to navigate it or a way that works for everyone, that does not ask too much from players, but also doesn't detract from the other investments that we need to make as a company," he added. "We also have tens of millions of people who we ask to spend $500 which is still an incredible sum of money. Those people that we asked to buy a console years ago, we still have an obligation to them to meet their expectations and to have them feel rewarded for which platform they chose.”

Xbox is far from the only company facing issues regarding memory shortage. PlayStation's hardware sales went down amidst the ongoing crisis, alongside price hikes. Then, Nex Playground, Nintendo, and Valve all increased hardware pricing, noting the memory shortage as either the main or one of the prevalent factors.

About the Author

Contributing Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Diego Nicolás Argüello is a freelance journalist and critic from Argentina. Video games helped him to learn English, so now he covers them for places like The New York Times, NPR, Rolling Stone, and more. He also runs Into the Spine, a site dedicated to fostering and supporting new writers, and co-hosted Turnabout Breakdown, a podcast about the Ace Attorney series. He’s most likely playing a rhythm game as you read this.



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/xbox-rethinking-strategy-around-project-helix-due-to-memory-crisis
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Here's Everything Announced During Nintendo's 2026 Summer Direct Presentation From The Zelda: Ocarina Of Time Remake To Deltarune Chapter 5 And More
item.content:encoded:
With Nintendo's Direct Presentation this morning, an argument can be made that Summer Game Fest 2026 has officially concluded. We can all take a breath and maybe a nap as we dream of all the games we will be playing later this year (mostly in September) and next year.

Below you will find a recap of everything shown during Nintendo's presentation, but you can find everything we wrote about as it relates to Summer Game Fest right here.

Every Game Shown During The 2026 Nintendo Direct Presentation

Below, we'll list each game in the order they appeared during the show, along with key details you need to know and their trailers. 

Rhythm Heaven Groove

  • Launches July 2 for Switch (but is playable on Switch 2).
  • The trailer showcases new games, co-op, and an RPG-inspired mode.

Onimiusha: Way of the Sword

  • The Onimusha sequel is coming to Switch 2 on September 25
  • Optional motion controls will be available exclusively for the Switch 2 version of the game.

Dragons Dogma 2: Dark Arisen

  • Dark Arisen is coming to Switch 2 on October 9.

Stellar Blade

  • Stellar Blade is coming to Switch 2 later this year.
  • Optional motion controls will be available exclusively for the Switch 2 version of the game.

Orbitals

  • The local co-op sanime inspired space adventure game launches September 3.

Rayman Legends Retold

  • Ubisoft's remake of arguably the best Wii U game made by a developer not called Nintendo is coming to Switch 2 October 1.
  • The revamped Kung Foot multiplayer mode can be played using GameShare locally and online

Big Walk

  • The co-op adventure game is coming to Switch 2 on August 4.

One Piece: Grand Gourmet

  • As opposed to pirating and adventuring, players will manage a restaurant in this pixelated management sim based on the popular anime and manga.
  • You will be able to optionally use mouse controls.
  • One Piece: Grand Gourmet launches on Switch and Switch 2 October 23.

Pokémon Pokopia Expansions

  • A free update will let players explore underwater.
  • Paid Expansion Pass DLC is also planned and will add three expansions. The first, Bubble Basin, launches in August.

Fire Emblem: Fortune's Weave

  • Fortune's Weave introduces the protagonist Cai who is trying to save his imprisoned father.
  • The next Fire Emblem launches September 17.

Ninjala 2: The Uncharted Planet

  • The Uncharted Planet is the first chapter of a two-part saga.
  • The sequel is a Switch 2 exclusive and launches Spring 2027.

DK Challenge Event

  • Starting June 9, players can play an assortment of Donkey Kong games to complete various challenges to unlock bonuses.

Donkey Kong Bananza Mario Content

  • Various Mario-themed costumes and themes have been added to Donkey Kong Bananza's DLC content today.

JuJutsu Kaisen Rumble: Survivaton

  • Survivaton is a Vampire Survivors-style game from the makers of Vampire Survivors that is JuJutsu Kaisen-themed.
  • It is coming to Switch 2 in 2026.

Lords Of The Fallen II

  • The soulslike action game is coming to Switch 2 Fall 2026.

Lies of P: Complete Edition

  • The soulslike action game is coming to Switch 2 August 6.
  • The Overture expansion will be included.

Devil May Cry 5 Devil Hunter Edition

  • The 2019 action game is getting ported to Switch 2 and will run in 60 FPS in both docked and handheld mode.
  • The game releases June 23.

Muramasa: Revenant Blades

  • Vanillaware's 2009 action game, which only released on Wii and Vita is getting ported to Switch and Switch 2.
  • Originally titled Muramasa: The Demon Blade, the Switch 2 version is called Muramasa: Revenant Blades.
  • This port will have improved 4K visuals, new game modes, added English voice acting, improved localization, and more.
  • The game releases early 2027.

Xenoblade Chronicles 1, 2 and 3 Switch 2 Enhanced Editions

  • All three Xenoblade Chronicles games are coming to Switch 2 with various visual and gameplay improvements.
  • Xenbolade Chronicles 1 is available today for Switch 2 and adds high-speed vehicles for navigate=ing the environment.
  • Xenoblade Chronicles 2 will be available digitally July 30 and Xenoblade Chronicles 3 releases digitally December 4. Physical versions of all the games will be available at later dates.

Xenoblade Genesis

  • Xenoblade Genesis is a brand new Xenoblade game and is a new beginning for the series.
  • Xenoblade Genesis launches in 2027 for Switch 2.

Nintendo Switch Sports Resort

  • The latest Nintendo motion control sports game will include Boxing, Table Tennis, Archery, Tennis, Volleyball, Bowling, Basketball, Golf, Thumb Wrestling, Jump Rope, Skateboarding, Power Cruising, and Prop Plan flying.
  • The game still takes place on Wuhu Island.
  • The game launches on Switch 2 October 22.

RuneScape: Dragonwilds

  • The next RuneScape  launches on Switch 2 September 15.

Hello Kitty Party Land

  • The game will feature 40 minigames and board games and feature Sanrio cahracters.
  • The game launches exclusively on Switch and Switch 2 on October 29.

Star Fox

  • A free demo for Star Fox will be available later today featuring the game's tutorial and one of the first levels.
  • Star Fox launches June 25 on Switch 2. 

Final Fantasy Resonance

  • Resonance is a new HD-2D Final Fantasy game.
  • The game is an adaptation (but not a port) of the story and world from the first arc of Brave Exvius, a Final Fantasy-themed mobile game that was shuttered last year
  • Final Fantasy Resonance releases on Switch 2 October 22.

Pikuniku 2

  • A sequel to the goofy puzzle game is coming to Switch 2 next year,

Dragon Quest Monsters: The Withered World

  • The Withered World is the next entry in the Dragon Quest Monsters series.
  • The game is coming to Switch and Switch 2 December 3.

The Duskbloods

  • The Switch 2-exclusive From Software game is getting a closed network test this Summer.
  • It is still set to launch later this year.

Splatoon Raiders

  • The single-player Splatoon Raiders is coming to Switch 2 July 23.
  • There will be a Direct with more info on June 30.
  • Splatoon Raiders-themed Joy-Con 2 controllers are coming soon.

Deltarune: Chapter 5

  • The next chapter, chapter 5, in the Deltarune saga releases on June 24.

Metaphor: ReFantazio

  • Metaphor: ReFantazio, the Atlus RPG from the makers of the Persona series is coming to Switch 2 on November 12.

Minecraft

  • Minecraft is coming to Switch 2 with all the content from the Switch version of the game (like the Mario biomes) and improved visuals.
  • Save content from the Switch version of Minecraft will work for the Switch 2 version.
  • Minecraft is coming to Switch 2 later this year.

Additional Game Switch 2 Release Reminders And Announcements

  • Atelier Karia: The Night Kingdom & The Guide Of Memories is coming to Switch 2 early 2027.
  • Warhammer 40,000: Space Marine is coming to Switch 2 holiday 2026.
  • Observer: System Redux is coming to Switch 2 June 18.
  • DayZ is coming to Switch 2 in 2026.
  • Metal Gear Solid: Master Collection Vol. 2 is coming to Switch 2 August 27.
  • Rise of the Tomb Raider is available for Switch 2 today.
  • Tales of Eternia Remastered is coming to Switch 2 October 16.
  • Snow Runner is available for Switch 2 today.
  • Fitness Boxing 3: Your Personal Trainer Nintendo Switch 2 Edition is coming to Switch 2 July 16.
  • Everbloom is coming to Switch 2 Spring 2027.
  • Final Fantasy XIV is coming to Switch 2 August 2026.

Kingdom Hearts Collection I-III For Switch 2

  • This new Switch 2 collection includes Kingdom Hearts - HD 1.5+2.5 ReMIX, Kingdom Hearts HD 2.8 Final Chapter Prologue and Kingdom Hearts III + Re Mind DLC. 
  • Kingdom Hearts Collection I-III For Switch 2 releases Oct. 8. Owners of the Switch 1 cloud version can purchase the Nintendo Switch 2 version at a discount.
  • A demo of Kingdom Hearts III + Re Mind DLC will be available later on Switch 2.

Kingdom Hearts IV

  • Nintendo aired a new Kingdom Hearts IV gameplay trailer during its Direct Presentation.
  • The game will coming to Switch 2 alongside PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X/S, and PC, but we still don't know when.

The Legend Of Zelda: Ocarina of Time Remake

  • The long-rumored remake of The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time is officially real.
  • The brief footage shown of the game showed Link in the game's new visuals asleep in his home in Kokiri Forrest, which is how the original Ocarina of Time begins.
  • In a press release Nintendo simply wrote, "The Nintendo 64 classic returns for a new generation in 2026, reborn exclusively for Nintendo Switch 2!"
  • Nintendo did not reveal a release date, but offered a 2026 window.

And that's everything revealed during today's Nintendo Direct Showcase. Thanks for reading this recap! What was your favorite announcement?



https://gameinformer.com/nintendo-direct/2026/06/09/heres-everything-announced-during-nintendos-2026-summer-direct
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Enjoy Vibrant Visuals With Minecraft's Native Switch 2 Release
item.content:encoded:
Creeping out later this year

A little game called Minecraft, you might have heard of it, is getting a native release on Switch 2 later this year.

You'll be able to enjoy the new(ish) 'vibrant visuals' display with enhanced lighting, while importing any creations you've already done on the Switch version. Unfortunately, there are no details as to whether current owners will be able to take advantage of any discounts, but we'll let you know if we hear any different.

"Survive the night or create a work of art in the iconic sandbox game! Build anything you can imagine, uncover mysteries, and face foes in an infinite world that’s unique in every playthrough. The Nintendo Switch 2 version features the Vibrant Visuals graphics upgrade, which brings improved visual elements, as well as the Super Mario Mash-Up Pack, where you can explore a custom Mario-themed world! The pack also features 15 tracks and 40 player skins inspired by the iconic Super Mario series.

Those who purchase the Nintendo Switch 2 version can also carry over their save data from the Nintendo Switch version. Play with friends across devices or in local multiplayer3 or connect with players online2 when Minecraft launches on Nintendo Switch 2 later this year."

Any interest in this one? What do you make of these so-called vibrant visuals? Let us know.

Nintendo Life’s resident horror fanatic, when he’s not knee-deep in Resident Evil and Silent Hill lore, Ollie likes to dive into a good horror book while nursing a lovely cup of tea. He also enjoys long walks and listens to everything from TOOL to Chuck Berry.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply


https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/06/enjoy-vibrant-visuals-with-minecrafts-native-switch-2-release
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: 'Stellar Blade' Brings Style And Sauciness To Switch 2 "This Year"
item.content:encoded:
Stellar physics

Korean dev Shift Up's hit action-adventure Stellar Blade is coming to Switch 2 this year.

As revealed in today's Nintendo Direct, the game came out on PS5 back in 2024 with Sony publishing. Featuring scantily-clad ladies battling an assortment of vile monsters, it caused quite a stir, too, with its pretty ladies and impressive combat, and was generally well received by critics, having landed on a Metascore of 81.

Here's some info from the PR blurb, plus some screens from the short Direct segment:

Step into the role of EVE, an elite warrior sent to reclaim Earth from mysterious forces. Experience intense combat and a twisting plot as you unravel the mysteries of Earth’s downfall in an immersive sci-fi world. Featuring a blend of cinematic storytelling and gameplay, Stellar Blade offers deep character progression with customizable skills, gear, and combat builds – which you’ll need during epic boss encounters. Further, players can experience support for Joy-Con 2 motion controls for activities, adding in an extra layer of immersion to moments away from combat.

No word on an exact release date beyond "2026" just yet, nor any info on a physical edition. We'll keep you posted as we get more details.

Played this on PC or PS5? Intrigued to see how this will hold up on Switch 2? Let us know down below.

Gavin first wrote for Nintendo Life in 2018 before joining the site full-time the following year, rising through the ranks to become Editor. He can currently be found squashed beneath a Switch backlog the size of Normandy.

  • Author Profile
  • Reply


https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/06/stellar-blade-brings-style-and-sauciness-to-switch-2-this-year
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Metaphor: ReFantazio Is Finally Getting A Release On Switch 2
item.content:encoded:
Fantaztic

Sega and Atlus brought the big guns to the Nintendo Direct today with Persona Metaphor: ReFantazio for the Switch 2.

Originally released in 2024, the JRPG garnered critical acclaim across the board for its visuals and mechanics, and fans have been itching to see it launch on Nintendo's console. Your wait is almost over – it will release on 12th November 2026.

Take that, GTA!

It seems like it'll be a straight port for this one, so don't go expecting any exclusive content or anything. Not that we need it, as such, it's a pretty massive game as it is.

Let's check out the key features:

- ATLUS' Newest Fantasy JRPG: A mysterious & exciting story unfolds in a world where people must fight their own anxiety. Join the high-stakes Royal Tournament that promises hours of captivating exploration and discovery.

- Award-winning Art Direction: Ride your “gauntlet runner” to faraway dungeons & towns across an expansive world. Discover beautiful landscapes  accompanied by captivating music, immersive UI, & anime cutscenes.

- Innovative Battle System: An exhilarating turn-based combat system with a blend of real time action. Party customization through "Archetype" powers provides the freedom to personalize your adventures.

- Time Management System: Go on quests, dungeon dive, & hunt for treasures by day. Strengthen relationships, build bonds, & increase virtues by night. Choose your actions wisely, time management is key to achieving your goal.

It's not Persona, but it'll do! What do you reckon? Will you pick this up? Let us know.

Nintendo Life’s resident horror fanatic, when he’s not knee-deep in Resident Evil and Silent Hill lore, Ollie likes to dive into a good horror book while nursing a lovely cup of tea. He also enjoys long walks and listens to everything from TOOL to Chuck Berry.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply


https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/06/metaphor-refantazio-is-finally-getting-a-release-on-switch-2
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Rise Of The Tomb Raider Gets Surprise Release On Switch 2 Today
item.content:encoded:
The 20 Year Celebration edition includes all DLC content

Rise of the Tomb Raider, the second in Crystal Dynamics’s Lara Croft reboots that was originally released in 2015, has been revealed for Switch 2 - and it’s out now.

Developed by Aspyr, who also handled last year’s Tomb Raider: Definitive Edition port, Rise Of The Tomb Raider: 20 Year Celebration features all previously released add-ons seen in other releases, including:

• All Season Pass Content & DLCs: Baba Yaga: The Temple of the Witch, Cold • Darkness Awakened, Blood Ties, Lara's Nightmare, Endurance Mode, and Co-op Endurance
• All Exclusives: 8 DLC Outfits and 7 DLC Weapons
• All Extras: 35 Expansion Cards, Extreme Survivor Difficulty, 5 Classic Lara Skins, and TR III Reimagined “Antarctica” Outfit

Tomb Raider: Definitive Edition also saw a surprise release last November, and though there’s no word on whether the third in the trilogy - Shadow of the Tomb Raider - will get the Switch 2 treatment, Aspyr discussed the possibility of all three entries in an interview late last year. Two down, hopefully one to go.

In the meantime, a remake of the very first Lara Croft adventure, Tomb Raider: Legacy of Atlantis, was recently confirmed to also launch on Switch 2 when it arrives early next year. It was also revealed to have used "AI-generated content" during development.

Are you looking forward to revisiting (or playing for the first time) Rise of the Tomb Raider? Let us know in the comments.

Matthew - an award-winning UK-based writer and editor specialising in guides and service articles - joined the Nintendo Life team in May 2026.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply


https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/06/rise-of-the-tomb-raider-gets-surprise-release-on-switch-2-today
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Nintendo Switch Sports Resort brings another 12 games to Wuhu Island
item.content:encoded:
Nintendo Switch Sports Resort has been revealed for, inviting you to Wuhu Island (not in a The Sims kind of way) to play 12 new and returning sports.

Switch Sports Resort is coming out on 22nd October exclusively for Nintendo Switch 2.

Using the Joy-Con 2 for motion controls, Switch Sports Resort will feature the following sports:

  • Boxing
  • Table Tennis
  • Archery
  • Tennis
  • Volleyball
  • Bowling
  • Basketball
  • Golf
  • Thumb Wrestling
  • Skateboarding
  • Power Cruising
  • Prop Plane

And you can also warm up with Jump Rope.

A bunch of these are returning from the last Switch Sports. Of the new ones, there’s some inventive new controller uses, such as needing mouse mode to play Skateboarding, or using Joy-Con as handlebars for the jet ski racing of Power Cruising.

You’ll be able to play once again as your Mii or a SportsMii, which has enhanced customisation options.

Online play will be included, with details on the player count for each game to be revealed at a later date.

Back with the last game, Jason said in our Switch Sports review, “Nintendo Switch Sports is a fun reminder of the Wii Sports craze, bringing back some classic sports and adding some new ones that make use of the Joy-Con and motion controls in more advanced ways. It’s all just a bit shallow, though, and difficult to wholeheartedly recommend unless you expect to play online a lot or constantly break it out for local head-to-head battles.”



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/09/nintendo-switch-sports-resort-brings-another-12-games-to-wuhu-island/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Orbitals brings its anime It Takes Two co-op action to Switch 2 in September
item.content:encoded:
Orbitals has a release date, the 90s anime riff on the beloved It Takes Two style co-op adventure set to launch exclusively for Switch 2 on 3rd September 2026.

The graphical style really shines through here, really capturing the look and feel of classic Japanese animation to go alongside the familiar looking split-screen co-op. The game sees explorers Maki and Omura setting out from their home to try and save it from an impending cosmic storm. They’ll be making use of separate abilities to support each others’ adventuring, using things like a grapple gun to pull out platforms, or cool lava with a water gun.

The game can also be played across multiple systems using GameShare, so you don’t have to play on TV or with a single Joy-Con.

Announced back at The Game Awards and then reappearing in a Partner Direct earlier this year, the game is in development by Tokyo-based indie team Shapefarm, but with assistance from animation house Studio Massket to craft its cutscenes. The game will have both Japanese and English audio dubs.



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/09/orbitals-brings-its-anime-it-takes-two-co-op-action-to-switch-2-in-september/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Square Enix whip out the defibrillator to bring back Kingdom Hearts 4 from the brink with a new trailer and promise of a PC release
item.content:encoded:
That company that makes the games about the plumber man, elf boy, and half-donkey half-gorilla held one of its "Directs" today, and lo and behold was the surprise showcase of a game that's been MIA for a surprisingly long time: Kingdom Hearts 4. The sequel was originally announced for the series' 20th anniversary in 2022, but has been noticeably absent for the past four years.

And what did it tell us? Well, truthfully, not all that much, unless you're a person like me who cannot be normal about this series and will be thinking about the implications of what was shown for months. For everyone else, there was a taste of the now even flashier combat, showing loveable, slightly less big-footed protag Sora zipping about on some magical chains as he fights maybe-Heartless big and small in not-Shibuya Shibuya. The young version of the original games' baddie Xehanort also made an appearance with a limp looking umbrella.

Notably, in a press release Square Enix do note that "Sora will once again journey to various worlds," an obviously vague point, but one that's been a bit of a question given how we've only seen this alternate Shibuya so far. The big thing here for us lot is that despite showing up in that Nintendo Direct, it is confirmed for PC now. More than that, it's also been confirmed to be fully multiplatform at launch (whenever that may be, Square did not deign to share this information just yet). Final Fantasy 7 Revelation, freshly announced at Geoff Fest, is taking a similar approach, a sign that Square truly are putting their multiplatform days behind them for the most part.

I imagine it'll be a while yet until Kingdom Hearts 4 actually gets into anyone's hands. Last year, Square cancelled the mobile spinoff Missing Link which would have bridged the gap between the previous games and the upcoming "fourth" game. Considering how much story was packed into the previous mobile game Union X, I can only imagine that the ever mysterious Tetsuya Nomura and co have had to figure out how to cram what would have been in Missing Link into Kingdom Hearts 4. It's probably safe to presume that this is part of what's taking it so long. I was in my mid-twenties when it got announced! Best move on from that before I have a crisis in the blog.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/square-enix-whip-out-the-defibrillator-to-bring-back-kingdom-hearts-4-from-the-brink-with-a-new-trailer-and-promise-of-a-pc-release
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Zelda: Ocarina Of Time Teaser Has The Internet Joking That Nintendo Is Entering Its ‘Hire This Man’ Era
item.content:encoded:
Today, during Nintendo’s big Summer Game Fest-adjacent Direct, the company officially revealed its much-rumored upcoming Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time remake. And a large portion of the internet reacted by joking that it looked a lot like those fan-created, often not-great-looking Unreal Engine remake videos of Zelda games, the ones that routinely have people saying in the comments that Nintendo should “hire this man.”

On June 9, Nintendo revealed a very short teaser for its upcoming remake of the Nintendo 64 classic The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time. We didn’t get to see much beyond a few seconds of what Link will look like in the remake, set to launch on Switch 2 sometime in 2026. But those few seconds were enough for people to start comparing this new, more realistic-looking version of the famous hero with how he’s often seen in fan-created remakes and trailers built using Unreal Engine or similar tools.

And sure, we’ve only seen a few seconds, and it could look much better when we see more of the game later this year, but that didn’t stop people from having some fun online or complaining about the art direction of the remake.

Over the years, these fan-made videos have become a meme online as they often lack the charm and character of the original Zelda games they are remaking, and still have people commenting below that Nintendo should “hire this man” or learn something from the video’s creator. With the new Link looking a lot like those versions, a lot of people are joking that Nintendo listened and did indeed hire that man after all. Some even pointed to Nintendo’s upcoming Star Fox remake as also featuring “hire this man graphics.” Others referenced the “Focus Up, M” meme, too.

2016: Nintendo Hire This Man
2026: Nintendo Hired This Man https://t.co/Uf7DyLp4rx pic.twitter.com/hoKNakKMhy

— Pinguis (@Pinguis54) June 9, 2026

why is ocarina of time in hire this man graphics

— William (@DooMYouTube) June 9, 2026

NINTENDO HIRE THIS MAN pic.twitter.com/js5sSkcZbI

— Johann (@LookAtMyMeat1) June 9, 2026

I’m sorry but I really can’t get over that it looks like Nintendo finally hired this man pic.twitter.com/Wr8qnRhcog

— Arty the Spectacular Bun 🐇 (@SuperiorGothBun) June 9, 2026

"Nintendo hire this man" ass trailer

— Alejandro (@Alejandro101524) June 9, 2026

You can’t even post the Focus, M video anymore they’re just making that the look

— august (@regularaugust) June 9, 2026

If you look at this and the new Star Fox Nintendo are seriously starting to hire That Man https://t.co/uhDn8Vr57V

— H20: 2nd Impact (@I__HATE_SOCCER) June 9, 2026

NINTENDO HIRE THIS MAN pic.twitter.com/0offXSlAOF

— Snugboy (@RealSnugboy) June 9, 2026

“Nintendo hire this man!” pic.twitter.com/PiBa6KYlLP

— G*rre (@springbris) June 9, 2026



https://kotaku.com/zelda-ocarina-of-time-teaser-has-the-internet-joking-that-nintendo-is-entering-its-hire-this-man-era-2000704454
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: PREVIEW: Blood Message Deserves Way More Attention Than It’s Getting
item.content:encoded:
Blood Message received an all-new trailer during the Summer Game Fest showcase, and I was lucky enough to follow that up with a behind-closed-doors hands-on preview at Play Days in Los Angeles. I was welcomed by NetEase and 24 Entertainment and given a brief introduction before diving into an early segment of Blood Message.

When I walked away, I turned to the developers in the room and pledged, ‘This is going to win awards.’ I was so taken aback by the quality, intensity, and emotion on display in Blood Message, and mused that it’s a single-player narrative title that could go toe-to-toe with the likes of Naughty Dog.

This is why I think Blood Message deserves way more attention than it’s getting.

Blood Message Will Blow Minds

Blood Message is shaping up to be one of the most impressive narrative-driven games in a hot minute. It’s a linear thing, which is refreshing, as it pulls away from some of the open-world fatigue gamers might be feeling these days.

What’s interesting is that Blood Message is rooted in reality. It takes place in 848 AD, during the late Tang Dynasty, and it sees players cross a war-torn landscape, exploring Chinese history and culture in a visceral and immersive way.

As players dive into Blood Message, they’re treated to some of the most jaw-dropping visuals, immense cinematic sequences, and authentic combat mechanics in recent years. When I played Blood Message, everything from the scripted chase sequences to the desperate fights for survival blew my hair back. I found myself connecting with the characters remarkably fast, and in a matter of minutes, I was emotionally invested.

The character models, set pieces, and vistas are all sumptuous and intricate. The smaller details make you stop and stare, and if this game doesn’t launch with a photo mode, I’ll start some kind of revolution, because I just wanted to take pictures of everything in Blood Message.

For instance, I entered some kind of textile store while sneaking my way past guards, and the way the light came through the roof slats, illuminating the dust hanging in the air, kicked up from the sandy floor and drifting across the half-lit interior, had me begging for some digital photography options.

I wasn’t able to find out how long the linear experience found in Blood Message will be, but the opening segment that I played sets up an epic story, which I later learned covers 1,000 miles across a brutal yet beautiful Chinese landscape.

The UI is almost non-existent, plunging players into a deep immersion, and the combat feels weighty, authentic, and frantic. The controls were easy to pick up, and the nature of the game as a third-person action title felt familiar within a matter of minutes. The animations were sublime, the Chinese voice acting was on point, and the audio palette, even through a set of tinny television speakers, was incredible.

I will proudly say that Blood Message was my number one game from the Play Days showcase in Los Angeles, and I was exposed to a lot of titles. I’m eagerly awaiting more information about this game, and if you are too, I’d firmly recommend you stay tuned and join the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out our preview of Arizona Sunshine from Summer Game Fest

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/blood-message-preview-deserves-way-more-attention/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
